## [1:53:07 - 1:56:10]

And you can also do that with different lead magnet ideas So you can say well, we've got different options and sometimes that will Dictate what kind of lead magnet the client or yourself needs to thereby build before it gets built before any energy gets put into it So you would do rapid fire testing through lead ads Then spend about a hundred dollars per ad set You'll have a pretty clear idea on which one of those is a winner Which one's going to get you the better results then you build out the funnel What we've done here is top of funnel We've set it up as a conversion objective campaign Optimizing for people opting into that lead magnet And we're excluding people that purchased and already opted in We want to test out a few different ad sets a few different targeting options because you don't want to put all your eggs in one basket And under each one of those Well in this campaign top of funnel, we have three ads that we're testing out and you can test out a lot more I'd recommend minimum three And then in our middle of funnel what we're trying to do is drive people to the sales page So we've warmed them up a little bit. We know they're interested. They've opted in Now it's time to drive them to the sales page and make them aware of this program this course Because maybe they didn't buy the trip wire. Maybe they didn't open the email sequence So traffic um to that sales page. This is a conversion objective as well Ideally you would optimize for view content or add to cart Um and you're not going to get many purchases from from middle of funnel Normally, usually it will mostly come from your bottom of funnel Um and you want to segment out retargeting stacks here So you want to do opted in day one to day five opted in day six to day 20 Opted in up to possibly 30 days and I would say, you know, test it out if you find that um by day 30 or 60 People are really not taking action Don't use them in in your funnel anymore use those people for better testing other concepts So what I mean by that is if someone opted in from day 30 to 60 That custom audience where you know, they're they're not taking the action I wanted them to they never did the action because they never moved in that funnel Days 30 to 60 you actually use them to better test other other funnel ideas or other creative ideas on them in a new campaign. So you would quietly in the background Run different ad ideas or different lead magnet rapid fires to that audience To see like what click-through rates, which one's gonna work so that When and if the day ever comes when this particular funnel starts to dry out or it gets ad fatigue or the creative needs to be changed Or new policies means that you can't run Um webinars anymore or whatever it is you have something else ready to go That's always a smart idea instead of going oh shit.
